programme.title: Desolation
programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Blood Glacier
programme.desc: Scientists discover that a red liquid leaking from a nearby glacier contains organisms that turn living hosts into deadly mutants.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Backcountry
programme.desc: A couple (Missy Peregrym, Jeff Roop) on a deep-wilderness hike become hopelessly lost within an aggressive black bear's territory.

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: American Fable
programme.desc: A young girl (Peyton Kennedy) learns that her beloved father is hiding a wealthy man in a silo to save their struggling farm.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Trench 11
programme.desc: In the final days of World War I, a soldier leads a team beneath the trenches to stop a German plot that could turn the tide of the entire war.

programme.title: The Hunt
programme.desc: While making an instructional video, hunters become the prey after they trespass on government land.

programme.title: A Lonely Place to Die
programme.desc: Five mountaineers stumble into a vicious kidnapping scheme after they rescue a child they find buried alive in the Scottish Highlands.

programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: I Trapped the Devil
programme.desc: Hoping for a joyful family reunion, Matt and his wife pay a surprise visit to the home of his estranged brother, Steve, to celebrate Christmas. To their shock, they soon learn that Steve has a hostage in his basement -- a man he claims is the devil.

programme.title: Entrance
programme.desc: Things lurking on the periphery of people's lives lead to horrific conclusions.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: The Tax Collector
programme.desc: Two enforcers for a crime lord face an uncertain future when an old rival reappears.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Rage
programme.desc: Following the kidnapping and murder of his daughter (Aubrey Peeples), a reformed criminal (Nicolas Cage) returns to his old ways to exact vengeance.

programme.title: Section 8
programme.desc: Sentenced to prison for avenging the murder of his family, a former soldier gets a second chance when a shadowy government agency recruits him for an assignment. However, he soon realizes that the very people who freed him are not what they seem.

programme.title: Desolation
programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Welcome to the Punch
programme.desc: When a notorious criminal is forced to return to London, it gives a veteran detective one final chance to bring the man to justice.

programme.title: VFW

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: The Numbers Station
programme.desc: Emerson, a disgraced CIA agent, guards Katherine, a code operator, in a top secret CIA installation in Britain. When heavily armed assailants lay siege to the compound, Emerson and Katherine have to stop the deadly plot before it's too late.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: Dog Eat Dog
programme.desc: Three ex-cons (Nicolas Cage, Willem Dafoe, Christopher Matthew Cook) find themselves on the run from the mob and the police after botching a plan to kidnap a baby for a large ransom.

programme.title: Area 407
programme.desc: The survivors of an airplane crash are trapped in a government testing area filled with predators.

programme.title: Baskin
programme.desc: Policemen (Gorkem Kasal, Ergun Kuyucu) face a night of horror in a subterranean labyrinth after answering a distress call.

programme.title: Beneath the Darkness
programme.desc: Teenagers discover a terrible secret when they break into the home of a mortician whose wife died two years earlier.

programme.title: Brawl in Cell Block 99
programme.desc: A desperate former boxer takes work as a drug courier and winds up in a jail where his enemies force him to commit acts of violence.

programme.title: Bushwick
programme.desc: Lucy emerges from a Brooklyn subway to find that her neighborhood is under attack by black-clad military soldiers. An ex-Marine corpsman, Stupe, reluctantly helps her fight for survival through a civil war as Texas attempts to secede from the U.S.

programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Grabbers
programme.desc: Residents of an island off the coast of Ireland learn that the only way to survive an invasion of blood-sucking aliens is to stay drunk.

programme.title: Kindred
programme.desc: Plagued by mysterious hallucinations, a pregnant woman suspects that the family of her deceased boyfriend has intentions for her unborn child.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: Re: Born
programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: The Shrine
programme.desc: The search for a missing teen in Poland leads a rookie reporter (Cindy Sampson) and her companions to an encounter with deadly cultists.

programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: A pride of lions has rewritten the rules - they have learnt to take down elephants.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: For most of Earth's history there was no life on land. But over 400 million years ago some tiny plants began an invasion from the water, closely followed by the first animals - the ancestors of millipedes and insects.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose tells the story of one of the greatest ever sea adventures, which transformed Captain James Cook into a national hero and dramatically changed the course of history. Two and a half centuries later, Captain Cook is still a household name, but his achievements are often misunderstood, contrary to popular perception, he did not discover New Zealand and Australia. Intrepid Rose follows his journey down under and uncovers the real story of Captain Cook.

programme.title: Starter for 10
programme.desc: Comedy about Brian, a 1980s working-class boy stumbling romantically and academically through his first year at Bristol University. He tries to achieve his lifelong ambition to appear on TV quiz show University Challenge and falls in love with his teammate.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: When Hyacinth and Richard go to stay in a country cottage for a couple of days, events take several unexpected turns. Richard goes in search of a dog, and finds himself caught up with more than he bargained for.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Margaret joins the local amateur dramatic society and takes Victor to a Bergerac party. Meanwhile, Margaret's mother manages to get stuck in the bath with wallpaper paste.

programme.title: Woolly Mammoth: Secrets from the Ice
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts reveals the natural history of the most famous of ice age animals - the woolly mammoth. Mammoths have transfixed humans since the depths of the last ice age, when their herds roamed across what is now Europe and Asia. Although these curious members of the elephant family have been extinct for thousands of years, scientists can now paint an incredibly detailed picture of their lives thanks to whole carcasses that have been beautifully preserved in the Siberian permafrost. Alice meets the scientists who are using the latest genetic, chemical and molecular tests to reveal the adaptations that allowed mammoths to evolve from their origins in the tropics to surviving the extremes of Siberia. And in a dramatic end to the film, she helps unveil a brand new woolly mammoth carcass that may shed new light on our own ancestors' role in their extinction.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: For most of Earth's history there was no life on land. But over 400 million years ago some tiny plants began an invasion from the water, closely followed by the first animals - the ancestors of millipedes and insects.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose tells the story of one of the greatest ever sea adventures, which transformed Captain James Cook into a national hero and dramatically changed the course of history. Two and a half centuries later, Captain Cook is still a household name, but his achievements are often misunderstood, contrary to popular perception, he did not discover New Zealand and Australia. Intrepid Rose follows his journey down under and uncovers the real story of Captain Cook.

programme.title: The Interview
programme.desc: David Hockney was one of the world's most successful and influential artists of our time. At 11, he wanted to be an artist. At 87, he was still creating new works - happiest when making 'joyful' art and always innovating with his iPad. His art has sold for millions. In a film originally broadcast in April 2025, ahead of his biggest show ever opening in Paris, the Bradford-born star talks to Katie Razzall about growing old, a recent visit from the King and his two big loves - smoking and painting.

programme.title: Face to Face
programme.desc: In an interview first broadcast in 1996, artist David Hockney talks to Jeremy Isaacs about painting, designing for the opera and his growing deafness.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Looking at the secret lives of the wildlife in one of Britain's favourite gardens. Shot by Charlie Hamilton-James, this film follows various animals, from the family of foxes that play after hours in the pleasure gardens to the toads in the Italian garden and the badgers that clear up the tea rooms at night. Set against the background of the gardening year, these stories reveal life behind the scenes at Heligan.

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: The BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, led by Ludovic Morlot, presents a concert celebrating spring’s extraordinary creative power as a season of renewal and rebirth, through evocative lighting and musical soundscapes, from Manchester’s Aviva Studios. Presented by Ben Gernon. Igor Stravinsky’s ballet score The Rite of Spring, which famously incited audience uproar in its 1913 Paris premiere, is an exhilarating start to the programme. With its asymmetrical rhythms, brutal harmonies and sonic aggression, it continues to fascinate audiences today and remains one of the most influential works of the 20th century. The UK premiere of Jessie Montgomery’s Chemiluminescence follows in calm contrast. This work for string orchestra introduces an atmosphere of contemplative beauty as science and the natural world are united through sound in the composer’s musical depiction of the creation of light from a non-light source. An evocative programme of musical concepts, exploring spring’s explosive destruction and radiant light.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: Two goliaths of 20th-century music, Ravi Shankar and Philip Glass, recorded the studio album Passages together in 1989. The result was a unique fusion of Hindustani sitar music with American minimalism. Tonight the album is performed live and in full for the first time, with Shankar's daughter Anoushka playing the sitar alongside a dazzling array of Indian soloists and the Britten Sinfonia, conducted by Karen Kamensek.

programme.title: In Concert
programme.desc: Concert featuring Indian musician Ravi Shankar, first shown in 1974. He performs just one song, entitled Rag Behag.

programme.title: South Asian Music at the BBC
programme.desc: To help mark 60 years of Asian broadcasting at the BBC, DJ Bobby Friction shares his love and knowledge of South Asian music with a journey back through the archives that celebrates some of the genre’s most important, enduring and spectacular acts from across the decades. With artists ranging from Lata Mangeshkar, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan and Ravi Shankar right through to Asian Dub Foundation, Naughty Boy and Apache Indian, Bobby’s selection highlights the vast range of musical styles and sounds, as well as the unbridled joy that feels built into so many of the performances that he’s personally selected.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Looking at the secret lives of the wildlife in one of Britain's favourite gardens. Shot by Charlie Hamilton-James, this film follows various animals, from the family of foxes that play after hours in the pleasure gardens to the toads in the Italian garden and the badgers that clear up the tea rooms at night. Set against the background of the gardening year, these stories reveal life behind the scenes at Heligan.

programme.title: Inside Classical
programme.desc: The BBC Philharmonic Orchestra, led by Ludovic Morlot, presents a concert celebrating spring’s extraordinary creative power as a season of renewal and rebirth, through evocative lighting and musical soundscapes, from Manchester’s Aviva Studios. Presented by Ben Gernon. Igor Stravinsky’s ballet score The Rite of Spring, which famously incited audience uproar in its 1913 Paris premiere, is an exhilarating start to the programme. With its asymmetrical rhythms, brutal harmonies and sonic aggression, it continues to fascinate audiences today and remains one of the most influential works of the 20th century. The UK premiere of Jessie Montgomery’s Chemiluminescence follows in calm contrast. This work for string orchestra introduces an atmosphere of contemplative beauty as science and the natural world are united through sound in the composer’s musical depiction of the creation of light from a non-light source. An evocative programme of musical concepts, exploring spring’s explosive destruction and radiant light.

programme.title: In Concert
programme.desc: Concert featuring Indian musician Ravi Shankar, first shown in 1974. He performs just one song, entitled Rag Behag.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: It’s the grand final of the series. Of the 28 teams that started the tournament, only two remain - but which will lift the trophy? Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. In the second semi-final, three fans of the Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy lock horns with the cunning of the Strategists, a team bound by their love of obscure European board games. They'll compete to draw together the connections between elements which at first glance seem utterly random.

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: Adventurer and journalist Simon Reeve heads to east Africa to uncover the stories behind the nation's favourite drink. While we drink millions of cups of the stuff each day, how many of us know where our tea actually comes from? The surprising answer is that most of the leaves that go into our everyday teabags do not come from India or China but are bought from an auction in the coastal city of Mombasa in Kenya. From here, Simon follows the tea trail through the epic landscapes of Kenya and Uganda and meets some of the millions of people who pick, pack and transport our tea. Drinking tea with everyone from Masai cattle herders to the descendants of the original white tea planters, Simon learns that the industry that supplies our everyday cuppa is not immune to the troubles of the continent - poverty, low wages and child labour.

programme.title: Andy Warhol's America
programme.desc: Charting Andy Warhol's response to the civil rights movement and the assassination of John F Kennedy, as well as other major historical events happening in America in the 1960s. We hear stories about Warhol setting up the Silver Factory and managing The Velvet Underground, and also how he faced his own personal nightmare: he was shot by a Factory associate.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes examines America's development into the art capital of the west between 1945 and the late 1960s, with the advent of abstract expressionism and pop art.

programme.title: imagine...
programme.desc: Twenty-something virtuoso multi-instrumentalist, singer and arranger Jacob Collier has managed to outdo The Beatles by winning Grammy Awards for each of his first four albums. Alan Yentob meets Jacob and musicians he has collaborated with, including Stormzy, Chris Martin and film composer Hans Zimmer. He also talks to music legends Quincy Jones and Herbie Hancock, who believe Jacob Collier is one of the most talented musicians on the planet today.

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: The astonishing story of the theft of two Turners on loan from the Tate in 1994 sparking a high-stakes international hunt across Europe from London to Belgrade, deep into the world of Serbian war lords.

programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Adrienne Murray meets the team using technology to preserve a historic warship in Sweden. Shiona McCallum has a round-up of the key innovations and trends on show at VivaTech in Paris. Suranjana Tewari heads behind the scenes of a driverless taxi service in Beijing. And as the summer music festival season gets underway, Andrew Rogers reviews some of the latest ear defender tech on the market.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Music and dance in China enjoy a fascinatingly long lineage dating back to at least 3000 BC. Ancient documents reveal countless forms of music and dance in connection with religious rituals, folk festivals and everyday life. Alastair explores some of the most iconic forms of ancient Chinese music and dance and also discovers how they are celebrated and kept alive today.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Talking Movies
programme.desc: To mark America's 250th anniversary, Talking Movies hears from leading figures in US media, film and culture who tell the BBC about some iconic American movies and what these films reveal about the country and the psyche of her people. American Psycho director Mary Harron talks about Martin Scorsese's The King of Comedy, while critic and film-maker Bilge Ebiri looks at Hitchcock's classic thriller North by Northwest. BBC News presenter Sumi Somaskanda reveals her passion for the prison drama The Shawshank Redemption and award-winning journalist Tim Teeman selects Steven Spielberg's Jaws. Talking Movies' host Tom Brook chooses the Robert Altman ensemble satire Nashville. Each contributor speaks about what their film choice means to them and how and why they feel it has become an American cinema classic. This special illustrates the American nation on the big screen and the influence it's had in bringing its movies to the world.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: Cook and food writer Rachel Khoo visits the cool of Sweden's capital city to continue her culinary journey. Rachel visits one of Stockholm's hippest restaurants, where rump of deer is on the menu. Back home, she's inspired by Sweden's traditional cinnamon buns and bakes her own savoury Swedish buns as well as cooking venison steak with celeriac puree, pickled blackberries and carrot petals and a lingonberry and raspberry marzipan tart, inspired by the Swedish lingonberry.

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: You could be a scroll away from finding a sperm donor. But at what cost? With fertility prices soaring and treatment difficult to access for some, same-sex female couples say they have little choice but to turn to social media to make a baby. Social media groups and apps are fuelling a cheap, easy and totally unregulated donor market. But it’s risky: outside of a sperm clinic, there’s no guarantee of health screening, and you’re not legally protected. And it’s not just a gamble for those wanting to get pregnant. According to the UK’s fertility regulator, men who donate sperm outside of clinics may also be breaking the law if they’re storing, processing or sending sperm. But what do they think of this? Meet the men travelling the world to donate sperm, and find out what happens when reporter Gemma Dunstan tries to order a sample online.

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: Having grown up just two hours from Belfast, Anna is excited to visit the city for the first time. She meets international boxing champion Carl Frampton as he shows her around his home city. Anna learns about Veda bread, a unique malt loaf staple in Belfast, before cooking for tourists at the birthplace of the Titanic. Carl puts Anna through her paces in the boxing ring and brings her to one of the city’s last traditional bakeries, where she discovers the local sweet treat, Fifteens.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: Guru Nanak Darbar Gurdwara in Gravesend in Kent – the UK’s largest Sikh temple – invites us to join their daily prayers and services. At 4.00am, the holy book, called the Guru Granth Sahib, is brought out from its overnight resting place, called the Sachkhand. It’s carried to the Main Diwan (prayer) Hall, where the first of the daily prayers are read. During the course of the morning, worshippers arrive at the temple to join a number of different prayers, including Japji Sahib (prayer from holy text), Asa di Var (hymn), Katha (sermon), Ardas (standing prayer) and Hukamnama (command). There is also the conclusion (Bhog) of a 48-hour prayer known as the Akhand Paath, a nonstop reading of the entire Guru Granth Sahib, Sikhism's sacred scripture. We also witness a core element of Sikhism called Sewa, or selfless service. Every morning at first light, volunteers arrive in the Langar (food) hall of the Gurdwara to prepare food for the congregation throughout the course of the day.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from the East of England Showground in Peterborough, with experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe on hand to help the teams.

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC’s television programmes. Whether you’ve seen something you’ve loved or loathed on the BBC, get in touch with your point of view. Email: pov@bbc.co.uk X: @bbcPoV Facebook: Search for BBC Points of View

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: As we enjoy the summer months, Pam Rhodes visits Ashburnham Place Retreat Centre in East Sussex to introduce a feast of much-loved hymns that explore the profound meaning of water in the Bible and in the expression of Christian faith. Surrounded by acres of ancient woodland and glorious lakes, Ashburnham Place provides a peaceful setting for Pam to reflect on the profound significance of water in the scriptures – from the story of creation to Jesus offering the water of eternal life. Pam also tells stories of composers for whom water proved a source of inspiration, with hymns and worship songs from across the UK, including Here Is Love Vast as the Ocean, performed by Unoma Okudo; I Heard the Voice of Jesus Say, from Our Lady's Church, York; and Like a Mighty River Flowing, from St George’s Church in Jesmond. Also featured are: O the Deep, Deep Love of Jesus, from St David's Church, Exeter; Rain, led by Noel Robinson at the Church of Christ the Cornerstone, Milton Keynes; and As the Deer Pants for Water, from Inverness Cathedral.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Imagine a warehouse bigger than a football pitch, full of some of science’s most important items. Near Swindon, Christina Trevanion visits the Science and Innovation Park to see some of the 400,000 objects stored there on behalf of England’s science museums. Among the most treasured items are personal possessions from the office of the late Professor Stephen Hawking, including a wheelchair, his specially adapted glasses and a figurine of his Simpsons character. Hoping for a breakthrough of their own are two teams of friends and colleagues browsing a bustling antiques fair in Exeter. In red are Vickie and Hannah, activities coordinators at the same care home, who watch the show every day with their residents! In blue are palliative care nurses Lindsay and Sarah, who both admit to being mad about dogs. With expert Ben Cooper in tow, the reds steer a course for a ship’s wheel, but Captain Cooper isn’t impressed with the high price. The blues alongside Gary Pe are true to their word, sidetracked by an art-deco-style dog statue and then distracted by the real thing in the arms of its owner!

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: When Miri Matteson returns home after eighteen years, can she integrate back into her old life? With a terrible event from her past hanging over her, it won’t be easy.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri sets about mending her friendship with Billy, after his terrible reaction to the truth of who she really is. She seems to have made progress until an encounter with his wife Anna takes a dark turn – and Miri winds up under the scrutiny of the local police. Meanwhile, having ended her relationship with Dom, Caroline is determined to relight the fire between her and Oscar. Unfortunately, no matter what she does and how hard he concentrates, Oscar’s body won’t respond to the attention. Could it have something to do with his inability to be honest with Caroline about her betrayal? Across town, Miri heads back to work at Nathan’s fish and chip shop, only to find that the shop is no more. Nathan is now running a food truck and, despite the positive spin he puts on it, it’s clear that hiring Miri was a terrible idea. Mandy is still trying to connect with Miri, who refuses to give her a second chance. But by the end of the day Miri is running out of friends and allies. Perhaps she will be forced to reconsider.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: Miri wakes up in a police cell once again – this time charged with arson. Although a mystery alibi quickly clears her name, the community will take a little longer to accept her innocence. After he secures her release, Miri agrees to meet the mysterious Scandinavian man who’s been following her since her return. He claims to know why Lara was so angry with Miri on the night that she died. His conclusion is far-fetched, but is it as far from the truth as it might first seem? Disappointed, Miri returns home to find that the vandals have struck again. It may be Oscar’s birthday, but the Mattesons aren’t going to get a break. To make matters worse, Billy is now a target too. Hurt and angry, Miri is still determined to move on and be judged for the person she is now, not for the terrible thing she did many years ago. Things come to a head as Miri confronts the community at a meeting held to run her out of town. As the meeting erupts in to turmoil, secrets and lies are laid bare, and it turns out that Miri isn’t the only one with a dark past.

programme.desc: Sketch show by Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou. The Brothers Pomodoro return to show off their new cookbook, and there’s a song about snakes.

programme.title: Ellie & Natasia

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: When an underwater base comes under attack, the Doctor and Clara must save the frightened crew and defeat an impossible threat. But what is behind these terrifying events? And can they really be haunted by ghosts?

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: On a remote army outpost, a fearsome alien warlord called the Fisher King sets in motion a twisted plan to ensure his own survival. The ripples will be felt around the universe. Is this chain of events inevitable? And can the Doctor do the unthinkable?

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Wendy paints a portrait of Lee's late father, Frank, and gives it to Lee and Lucy as a present. She is expecting them to love it, but that would require the painting to be not just competent but good. Once it’s hanging on their wall, Lee and Lucy must contrive to lose the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the stupid mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she reads it.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira receives a gift from an old friend, and it turns out to be a cosmetics kit to sell around town. David, excited at the possibility of selling enough to win a car, encourages Moira to do a lunch where they can sell to all the locals. Johnny, also concerned with bringing in an income, must find a way to get to Elmdale in the hope of collecting unemployment insurance, while Alexis thinks her new boyfriend Ted is too nice and tries to turn him into someone her family will respect.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny reluctantly agrees to speak at the funeral of a local man he doesn’t even know. Meanwhile, Alexis can’t seem to get close to Ted, as his love of animals gets in the way. David, on the other hand, finally gets up close and personal with Stevie, crossing the line from friend to friend with benefits.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: After spending a night together, David and Stevie’s relationship seems to be more then they had ever expected it to be. Meanwhile, Johnny and Moira invite themselves to Roland and Jocelyn’s annual Hawaiian bash and discover that they really aren’t that different. Hoping to show off their perfect relationship, Alexis and Ted throw a couples dinner party, but things go south when Alexis’s relationship with Mutt seems to be more than she says it is.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Dermot and Maria's wedding is just days away and tensions are running high in the Brown household. The groom is getting cold feet. His best man Buster can't find anything good to say about Dermot in his speech. To cap it all Agnes Brown is mystified as to why her daughter Cathy has given her a book on homosexuality. Is she trying to tell her mother that one of the family is gay? When Dermot's pre-marriage nerves get the better of him, he does what he always does when stressed. He locks himself in the cupboard. Father Quinn is bemused to have to give Maria and Dermot their pre-marriage talk in slightly unusual circumstances - with the mother of the groom in attendance and the groom in the cupboard. If Dermot won't reappear for the priest, how on earth is Agnes going to persuade him to come out in time for his wedding? Meanwhile, Agnes Brown goes on a hunt for the homosexual in the family. Eventually she realises who it is. With help from Cathy and a greetings card, Agnes reassures them, and herself, as only she can.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes Brown thinks everyone has gone mad. Father Quinn is losing his faith as fast as he loses parishioners. Newlyweds Dermot and Maria will not stop fighting. And Grandad has decided that he would like to witness his own funeral. He is keen to hear all the nice things people will say about him at his funeral while he is still alive to enjoy them. Maria tells Mrs Brown why she and Dermot have been arguing so much. She thinks that they should move out of the Brown household, but Dermot is resisting. He is also scheming with Buster to make some quick cash so that he can afford a first home for him and Maria. The Browns decide to make Grandad happy by faking his funeral, but their plans start to go awry when Mrs Brown mistakes two Mormons for the men who are supposed to be delivering Grandad's coffin. In the event, Grandad is horrified to find that no-one has a kind word to say about him after all, and the mourners get a shock when the corpse comes back to life. Only a quick-thinking Mrs Brown can save the day.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists – and a stubborn Ken – in order to secure Ken a doctor’s appointment on the same day that he is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and embarks on a journey to track down the elusive Brian – Ken’s oldest friend, with whom he lost touch with years ago – in order to invite him to Ken’s surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard struggles with finding the right time to tell Ken his big news as the two of them spend the day touring Forres in search of the ideal spot to scatter Jean’s ashes.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: The Bluestone 42 team experience a tough roadside op, but it's the new padre, Mary, who really grabs the attention of bomb disposal expert, Nick. Written by Richard Hurst and James Cary.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Comedy drama about a British bomb disposal detachment in Afghanistan. Nick tries to find some decent food that he can use to seduce Mary. Simon gets into trouble on roadside ops.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: After Bluestone 42 join a midnight raid on a bomb factory and rescue a kidnapped Danish aid worker, they find more than they bargained for. Simon tries to get a book deal for his memoir.

programme.title: Rooney
programme.desc: The 2022 documentary film giving unprecedented access to the life and career of one of football’s greatest strikers. Wayne Rooney recounts stories from some of the most important moments of his life, with testimonies from the people closest to him, including family, friends and his wife Coleen. From his time as a young boy growing up in the Liverpool district of Croxteth, through to his teenage life where his emerging stardom led to intense scrutiny from the British press, the film places Rooney’s professional career under a microscope, journeying through some of the highest and most challenging moments in the life of England’s record goal scorer. Footballing greats such as David Beckham, Gary Neville, Rio Ferdinand and Thierry Henry illustrate first-hand accounts of Rooney as a teammate and an opponent. Meaningful testimonies from the likes of Rooney’s first Premier League manager, David Moyes, and his first England manager, Sven-Göran Eriksson, describe the seemingly unlimited potential they saw in Rooney at such a young age.

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo
programme.desc: This film looks at the rivalry between Lionel Messi and Cristiano Ronaldo, which transcended clubs, countries and competitions for over a decade, to become the defining story of modern football.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Relive the 2022 World Cup final between Argentina and France.

programme.title: Desmond Armstrong: America's World Cup Pioneer
programme.desc: Desmond Armstrong was the first US-born black player to represent the United States men's national football team at a World Cup. He was part of the squad that did the unthinkable: qualifying for Italia 90 at a time when the US had no professional league and football wasn't taken seriously. This documentary explores how he got there, the racial tensions of that era and his work as a youth academy coach in Antioch, Nashville, making the sport accessible to immigrant communities against a backdrop of heightened immigration enforcement.

programme.title: The Trials of Pa Salieu
programme.desc: After prison, Coventry musician Pa Salieu returns with renewed purpose and takes the stage at Glastonbury. While he fights to rebuild his career, can music save him again?

programme.title: P Diddy: The Rise and Fall
programme.desc: Yinka Bokinni explores the life of Sean 'Diddy' Combs, who has been jailed on prostitution-related charges She meets some of his inner circle to find out more about him.

programme.title: Rivals: Messi v Ronaldo

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall meets one of the planet's ultimate killers, the saltwater crocodile, as the Deadly 60 team land in Australia's Northern Territories. A rogue croc is terrorising local fisherman, so it is time to lend the park rangers a hand in catching and relocating this prehistoric monster. Steve heads deep into an abandoned mine on a ghost hunt. He is on the search for ghost bats, awesome creatures with huge fangs, that drop on to their prey out of the night sky. Scary stuff, and a first for the Deadly team as these bats have never been filmed in the wild before.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey attempts to clear Billy’s name. Kim continues to avoid Denise out of guilt.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to end the chaos and misery that snowfall brings to Britain every year, using a combine harvester, some basic engineering skills and a quick trip to Norway. Jeremy is on the test track in the Audi RS5 and the BMW M3, and there's a rather surprising guest in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Dr David Graham is online and looking for love. He seems to have it all: the looks, the job, and the charisma. On paper, he’s Kilmarnock’s most eligible bachelor. But when the town’s former beauty queen Abbie Draper starts to receive messages from him after he treats her grandfather in hospital, she gets the feeling he might be too good to be true. She turns online sleuth and starts to uncover a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just don’t add up. Dr David isn’t just a bad boyfriend; something more sinister is afoot. Abbie starts trying to figure out who David Graham really is and why he is doing this.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Dr David’s victims begin comparing their stories, and it becomes clear they are dealing with a sophisticated catfish. But nothing could prepare them for the shocking truth of the perpetrator’s true identity, which leaves the women with more questions than answers. But when beauty queen Abbie takes these findings to the police, they are unable to do anything about it, and the Catfish of Kilmarnock remains free to target more and more victims. Abbie can’t walk away, and steps up her investigation over months that turn into years. As she continues to track the catfish, the scale of their criminality comes into focus, with victims spread across the country and dating back over a decade. This is one of the UK’s most prolific ever catfish, and they are not done yet.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: As criminal investigations take hold, the victims finally have justice, but they are still left urgently searching for answers as they try to rebuild their lives. What drives someone, with a seemingly normal life, to throw it all away to terrorise countless women from the same town? With no clear motive and no explanation from the person in the frame, this very modern true crime tale now turns to those closest to the perpetrator, who are also trying to understand the mystery behind the repeated cycles of offending. As the beauty queen and the catfish’s lives become ever more entangled, lines begin to blur, loyalties are tested, and Abbie finds herself questioning everything as history repeats, and the moral complexity of crime and punishment in the internet age is laid bare. For the many victims, the question remains: what drives someone to become one of the UK’s most prolific catfish - and why can’t they stop?

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: Twenty-year-old Alexander McCartney, already known to police, is arrested and his devices seized after a 12-year-old girl in Scotland reports a serious catfishing abuse incident online. McCartney gives a ‘no comment’ interview while in custody, and without enough physical evidence to charge him, he is released on bail. Back home at his parents’ house, McCartney’s bail conditions are supposed to prevent him from using devices that can connect to the internet. Meanwhile, a second report comes in to the police about another catfishing abuse victim linked to online activity from McCartney’s home address. This time, it involves a young girl in New Zealand. As the police continue to gather up enough physical evidence to charge McCartney, the Cyber Crime Centre completes the analysis of one of his devices, revealing a prolific online predator at a scale never before seen by police in Northern Ireland.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: With hundreds of thousands of images of child abuse discovered on McCartney’s devices, the race is on to get him remanded into custody. McCartney is rearrested in the early hours of the morning, at which time police discover McCartney’s phone on his bedside table, where his final victim was abused on Snapchat just hours before. The US Department of Homeland Security notify the police in Northern Ireland of two more catfishing victims, this time in the United States, and a multiagency international investigation is triggered. As McCartney is remanded into custody, police across the world work together to identify and safeguard the thousands of victims discovered on McCartney’s devices. Police are especially keen to identify and locate a 14-year-old girl in Oregon, whose images McCartney used to catfish hundreds of other children. With McCartney due in court to plead guilty to charges of making indecent of images of children and blackmail, the case takes a different turn when Homeland Security finds an obituary page for Cimarron Thomas, one of his victims in West Virginia.

programme.title: Teen Predator/Online Killer
programme.desc: As police piece together the last few minutes of Cimarron’s life, they realise McCartney was still online threatening her when she tragically shot herself. Police and prosecutors move quickly to establish if there’s a case for manslaughter. Police from Northern Ireland travel to West Virginia to conduct the manslaughter investigation, where they learn that Cimarron’s own family had no idea why she took her own life. In a tragic turn, they learn that 18 months after her death, Cimarron’s father had also died by suicide. In order to charge McCartney with manslaughter, the police have to prove that he foresaw the risk to her life. As they comb through the cyber evidence from his devices, they build a case for manslaughter and charge him. The case sets new legal precedent in the UK. More than five years after the police investigation began, McCartney is finally convicted and sentenced.

programme.title: Liar: The Fake Grooming Scandal
programme.desc: Twenty-two-year-old Ellie Williams was jailed for lying about being trafficked and raped by a vicious Asian grooming gang. Now, for the first time, the truth is laid open.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two teens missing from care: a 13-year-old who has failed to return after school, and a risk-taking 18-year-old who has not been seen for five days.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: After a spate of unfortunate accidents, Pancake is convinced that rubbing a lucky icicle will bring him good luck but panics when it’s rubbed away, until his friends step in.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Dad has left Cosima in charge for the day. It seems like an excellent opportunity to have a massive party! What could possibly go wrong?

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Spicy directs a show starring Sunny and the eaglets. Weasy wants to join in, but Spicy worries he will overdo it and refuses to give him a role. Weasy forces his way onto the stage, convinced she will recognise his talent.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Forest inspectors pay the forest a visit to check its super-happy status. Friendlyface sends the heroes on outlandish quests to guarantee results.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings play with a baby ostrich that Grizzy found outside.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog creates chaos in a loo roll factory, leaving Duck covered in toilet paper. Duck just wants to fix the machine, but Frog is convinced he’s a spooky mummy!

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: The Rising Volt Tacklers reunite and prepare to take to the skies once more.

programme.title: Winx Club - The Magic Is Back
programme.desc: Bloom is torn when she learns she must take a test at Alfea to determine whether she is a fairy or a witch. The Trix take advantage of her doubts, pushing Bloom closer to despair.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Every single Gladiator gets their revenge on Nitro. He thinks he is filming a science show, but phoney interruptions and bogus camera hiccups lead to an epic pie pandemonium finale!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Joel attempts something that has never been done before for his final epic challenge – climbing Portsmouth’s Spinnaker Tower! Back in the studio, Joel tries every job on the show, and we look back at his best bits.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
programme.desc: When struggling film-maker Dean moves into a rental property, he quickly discovers he is not alone. Marcel, an adorable one-inch-tall shell, already lives there with his grandmother Connie and their pet lint Alan. Once part of a sprawling community of shells, Marcel and Connie live alone as the sole survivors of a mysterious tragedy. Taken with his unlikely new friend, Dean starts filming Marcel and his grandmother, igniting a meteoric rise to internet fame that changes their lives forever. But will Marcel’s newfound sense of possibility and a throng of superfans be enough to track down his long-lost family?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Adam Frost’s brand new garden starts to take shape, it’s time to get some shrubs in the ground and construct a path. He also makes a start on planting up a potted herb garden and shares some ideas on what to grow against a wall, whether it be sunny or shady. At home in Devon, Carol Klein is mixing it up with flowers and edibles, where sweet peas and cosmos happily mingle with beetroot, basil and beans. And at West Dean Gardens in Chichester, Jamie Butterworth reveals the secrets of growing that border belle, the delphinium. In Birmingham, a lover of roses has created a garden full to brimming with beautiful blooms, while a gardener in Norwich shares her contemporary cottage garden that became a refuge when she needed it most.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: 50 American Music Icons at the BBC
programme.desc: As America celebrates 250 years of Independence, we celebrate 50 of the biggest names in modern American music and their performances over the years at the BBC. It’s a playlist of acts covering 70 years of classic hits, including the most influential and successful artists, bands and songwriters who made the journey from the US charts to international superstardom and also took time to appear on a UK audience's TV screens on their way. Featuring artists ranging from Beyonce and Bruce Springsteen, Ella Fitzgerald to Nirvana, Liberace to Johnny Cash, Louis Armstrong to Lady Gaga, and Carole King to Bruno Mars, this collection highlights the incredible range of genres and songs that US artists have given the world, and why so many of them are – to quote Tina Turner – simply the best.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: A look back at the day’s play at Wimbledon.

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights of the second game of the T20 series between England and India.

programme.title: Godland
programme.desc: In the late 19th century, a young Danish priest is sent to Iceland with a mission to set up a parish in a remote part of the country. Faced with an ambivalent reception by less-than-welcoming locals, he finds that his zeal, faith and sense of reality start to drift as he ventures deeper into the Icelandic landscape. In Danish and Icelandic with English subtitles.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Joe Swash goes in search of his Italian roots. Along the way, he finds stories of murder, banditry, an inspiring political activist and a family bar in southern Italy.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden visits Bodnant Garden in Snowdonia to explore a Himalayan valley amid the mountains of north Wales. She meets Phyllis, who is treading in her grandfather's footsteps. At nearby Bodysgallen Hall, an unexpected view takes her back to childhood holidays. And we hear the story of the building of the Snowdon Mountain Railway.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Charlotte Smith and Joe Crowley mark the 50th anniversary of the Viking Way, exploring the people, wildlife and history connected by Lincolnshire's famous long-distance footpath. They uncover evidence of Viking settlers, meet the volunteers who maintain the route, visit the Ramblers' Church and witness efforts to protect water voles. Adam Henson explores high-tech strawberry farming, and John Craven launches Countryfile's annual photographic competition.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Lizzie Schofield and Brian Cunningham are starting new projects and checking on the progress of planting carried out in previous years. Lizzie is in her herb garden, which she renovated in 2024, assessing what has worked and what needs to be refreshed. Meanwhile, Brian is checking on the apple trees he planted in the spring as examples of espalier shaping and pruning. Lizzie begins plans in the garden for cut flowers that will give her foliage alongside the blooms already beginning to take shape, and Brian is planting more perennials in the terraced patio area. There are all the usual handy hints, as well as a look at what plants are good for attracting birds to your plot.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: The last programme of the spring series, Dougie and the team are at Ingliston for The Royal Highland Show. Cammy catches up with a professional sheep dresser as he makes finishing touches before showing his Bleu du Maines. As Landward celebrates fifty years on air, Dougie looks back on show coverage from the archive. Rosie gets a taste of Aberdeenshire in the food and drink hall showcasing artisan producers, Arlene visits the honey tent to find about the complexities of judging the amber nectar, Cammy’s hoping to win a red rosette in the show ring with his Jersey dairy cow. And Dougie steps into the main ring to follow the spectacular grand parade.

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Charlotte Bronte's classic about an orphan girl who grows up to become a governess in a gloomy manor in Yorkshire, where she falls in love with the mysterious Edward Rochester.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani begins a four-part journey along England’s east coast, exploring the wildlife, landscapes and communities adapting to life on the edge of the sea. Her first stop is Northey Island in Essex, an internationally important refuge for threatened birds, including curlew, lapwing and dunlin. With rising sea levels putting increasing pressure on coastal habitats, Anita discovers an innovative conservation project using retired Thames barges to help protect the island’s future. She also meets the volunteers working to safeguard vulnerable ringed plover nests using a simple but effective solution. Anita also delves into the Countryfile archives to revisit some of the projects helping to create more space for nature to thrive.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow visits one of the grandest, historic houses in Wales, Powis Castle. Duncan Campbell discovers a very rare silver tobacco box from the 1600s, and Serhat Ahmet examines a porcelain egg with a royal connection. Meanwhile, Paul Atterbury is taken back to the earliest days of Doctor Who, while Alexandra Aguilar values a jade ornament bought at a car boot sale for 50 pence. Geoffrey Munn uncovers a jewellery collection ‘of the highest quality’ with rubies and diamonds dating from the 1700s.

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Rob Rinder and Monica Galetti travel to Turkey to work at Argos in Cappadocia, a hotel built into a network of ancient caves. Combining history with modern luxury, Monica finds herself brushing cave ceilings with housekeeping before flying above in a hot air balloon. Meanwhile, Rob helps with the finishing touches of a new spa. With each room providing surprises and inviting curiosity, Rob seeks further education on the history of the caves as Monica practises centuries-old traditional cooking techniques.

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: A team of detectives arrive in the market town of Elgin tasked with investigating the brutal murder of a 32-year-old mother of two. On 16 November 2023, Lucretia ‘Kiesha’ Donaghy’s body is discovered in her home after worried friends are unable to reach her. She has been victim of a frenzied attack. We follow the detectives' every move as they search for key evidence and piece together information to uncover the truth of who killed Kiesha. The case quickly unfolds into a complex investigation which demands relentless focus and all of the team’s expertise. Kiesha’s aunt Rachel and her best friend Bob share memories of her vibrant personality, the struggles she faced, and the heartbreak of losing her. With intimate access to the detectives and Kiesha’s loved ones, this episode reveals the personal impact of her murder and the dedicated work involved in seeking justice.

programme.title: Blade Runner
programme.desc: The 2007 final cut of Ridley Scott's sci-fi classic. Los Angeles, 2019. Mankind has succeeded in creating replicants - near-perfect humanoids superior in intelligence even to humans - but as a result of a mutiny on a human colony in outer space, it is now illegal for unlicensed replicants to be on Earth. The punishment, if they are caught, is death. Four replicants have escaped and fled back to Earth, and android hunter or 'blade runner' Deckard is charged with identifying and 'retiring' them...

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: A look back at the day’s play at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Teddy Pendergrass: If You Don't Know Me
programme.desc: Documentary charting the life, music and towering achievements of soul singer Teddy Pendergrass, from his origins and early life in Philadelphia, through his early successes with Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes and his solo superstardom and sex symbol status to his later life and continued success after overcoming the car crash that rendered him quadriplegic.

programme.title: Madonna & Graham

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross uncorks an episode of the show fizzing with fun and excitement at the South of England Showground in West Sussex. Among the stalls at Ardingly Antiques Fair, he quizzes punters about a mystery metal object. While they ponder its purpose, two teams of antiques hunters search for three profitable items to take to auction. On the red team are friends Alex and Ollie, skippered by expert Jonathan Pratt. Against them are the blues, pals Lesley and Rachel, who are shopping with expert Kate Bliss. Early in their shop, Kate spots a silk handbag that looks as if it's art deco but on closer inspection is revealed to be something later, so it goes back in the cabinet. Over with the reds, the lads spy an Edwardian desk set, but Jonathan doesn’t think it’s very practical, so it fails to make the grade. With minutes left on the clock, one team relaxes with a cake and a cuppa, while the other rushes to bag their last buy. In Buckinghamshire, auctioneer Hugo Lemon is ready and waiting to sell the teams’ items, but can he hammer out profits for either of them?

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob travel to Mid Wales to fish for wild carp in some equally wild and wonderfully picturesque lake locations. They've had success catching both mirror and common carp in previous trips, but trying to net a ‘Welsh wildie’ will require a different approach. Wild carp are the oldest strain of the species we have in the UK, and there are tales from the middle ages of monks tending carp ponds. Starting off high in the Welsh hills, Paul and Bob spend their first day fishing an ancient glacial lake. Bob has some new carp gear that he is keen to use and hopes will make a difference to his fishing. During this trip, Bob and Paul also meet Mick May and Marina Gibson, who together have started a charity called Cancer and Pisces, which offers fishing as therapeutic respite for people with cancer.

programme.title: Newsround

programme.title: Hacker’s Ruff Guide to the World Cup
programme.desc: In the World Cup's knockout stage, one thing is clear: teams are exiting the competition, so everyone's nerves are going to be shredded! But don't worry, as CBBC's very own Rufferee Hacker is on hand to offer his top tips on how to cope with the nerves of being a footy fan when you long for glory!

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working overnight in a museum on his own. To keep him company, Duck makes a Frankenstein Frog, which soon runs rampage. It’s up to Frog to save the day!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck and Frog work on a farm, Frog makes the perfect scarecrow. However, he gets covered in super-strong fertiliser and becomes a giant, monster-sized, chaos-causing Frog!

programme.title: Odd Squad
programme.desc: An international high-tech organisation run by kids, which investigates all things strange, weird and, most importantly, odd.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gross Alert! Exploring the weird and wonderful world of medicine. From sneezes and snot to pimples and poo, nothing escapes the Doctors' crazy experiments!

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve is going large as he focuses on the biggest animals to have ever walked the earth. In his dino den, he starts by meeting the Argentinosaurus - an animal so large that even its poo was deadly! But if you thought that was the biggest ever dinosaur, then think again. With stunning graphics and epic stunts, featuring a piano smash, a fire hose hose-down and the most powerful heart in the animal kingdom, Steve crowns his king of all beasts.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: A totally wicked song about gravity, a T-rex going to see a doctor, vampires on holiday, and Sir Isaac Newton riding a rollercoaster - welcome to the bizarre world of Horrible Science!

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Meet the zoo’s powerful animals as Sonika the Asiatic lioness gets a grip in a tug-of-war and Tyler takes on Marion the sulcata tortoise in a contest of strength.

programme.title: Gym Stars
programme.desc: Birmingham prepares for the Commonwealth Games.

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Meet the Tottenham Hotspur academy players. Lia Lewis picks her Three of the Best, and Dominic Solanke takes on 11-year-old Konan in Skill Showdown.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: The Kwapongs make a bug jungle, Chris Howarth makes sand art waves, and Rob Biddulph shows how to draw a furry monster!

programme.title: Cooking Buddies
programme.desc: Kicking off the series in Buddy’s den, the gloves are on as Buddy and Conker are joined by Jackson, a young boxer who wants to learn a recipe that can pack a punch to keep his energy up! Buddy knows that Jackson loves a big-stacked sandwich, so what better than a homemade fish finger sandwich topped with crunchy lettuce and a simple, delicious, two-ingredient sauce, all packed into a soft bun?! Learning to cook means that you can make your meals exactly how you like them, so Jackson chooses cod and Buddy uses salmon, and things get messy when they breadcrumb the fish! Taking time out from the kitchen, Buddy is kept on his toes by Jackson as he gives him a lesson in boxing. Buddy learns to be light on his feet, throw a punch and read his opponent... Join Buddy, Conker and Jackson in this tasty episode of Cooking Buddies that certainly packs a punch! You can get all of Buddy’s recipes and LOADS more at bbc.co.uk/food

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro and Apollo prank Dynamite, turning her into a singing banana! The other Gladiators have fun behind the scenes and pranking the audience at Sheffield Arena.

programme.title: Octonauts

programme.title: Big Lizard

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Nugget finds a lost mini alien and adopts him as a pet, until the others convince her to let it return home. But with his flying saucer broken, how can they get him back to space?

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Frog tries to bring a touch of class to his new job in a cafe, but Duck's desire to wash each plate to polished perfection is holding him up.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes are on a quest to find the vault containing the Amulet of Answers and protect it at all costs. But the Riddlemaster is back, and he manages to trap them in his castle, tricking Herbert into handing the Amulet of Answers over to him. Now he has all the answers to every question ever asked, and the heroes must find a way to outwit him - even though he knows what they are going to do next!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings

With fast-swimming prey like tuna and mackerel, mako sharks need to move quickly. But how do they reach such meteoric speeds? Steve first snorkels with these lightning-quick sharks off the coast of San Diego in California, before getting under their skin in his shark lab to reveal their hidden speed secrets.
